reviews.review_description: I had played the full trillogy and have got to say, i loved it, a great addition to the game was the graphics, and also the addition of actual music within the missions, which IEYTD 1 didnt fully have, another intresting change that also made sense is the replacement of speedrun scorpions statues, with speedrun watches, another cool addition is the live radio broadcasts throughout completing missions, one downside however is that there are only 6 missions in total, while IEYTD 1 had 7, I infact enjoyed the game so much, i went and 100%ed it, i had alot of fun doing it to, the game also captures the theatrical and spy atmosphere of the game, and overall a well written story, and No, you dont exactly need to play the 3 games in order, however playing the games in order will help regognise some of the characters from the previous games and understand what happened previously. Overall, a 10/10 game

reviews.review_title: Honestly one of THE Best Games
reviews.review_description: I can’t say how many times I’ve played. This game is so addicting it’s not even funny. The story line, continuing off the first game is what I love. Every level has an even better set of secrets as you go. To the creators, thank you once again.

This game, like the other games in the IEYTD trilogy, is an absolute masterpiece. The immersion that I feel when playing this game is genuinely astronomical. The voice acting, the world building, the puzzles, everything is just so masterfully crafted. I usually only write reviews for games that I had a funny experience with, whether that’s good or bad, and this game has just entered that category for a couple of reasons, even though I’ve already played through it what’s probably been hundreds of times. 
Firstly, this game/trilogy ACTUALLY has me responding to the name Phoenix, (the codename of the protagonist). When I hear someone talking about somebody named Phoenix, or the place, my brain sends me the same signal that it does when I hear my own name. Once when someone said it, I actually turned and asked what they needed. Most people would find this concerning, but I just take it as an example of how good the game is. 
Secondly, just today, I was sitting in the living room with my little sister, reading a book. She was watching some cartoon called “Dogs In Space.” I was simply minding my own business, when I suddenly heard John Juniper’s voice, (the antagonist of the game), and I genuinely felt a fight or flight response kick in. I actually dropped my book and shot up from the chair, before realizing that Actor Boy’s voice had been coming from the TV, in which Wil Wheaton apparently voices one of the dogs. I was genuinely ready to fight for my life, and was momentarily shocked to not see a laser cage decending from the ceiling. 
Anyways, this trilogy has genuinely become my way of coping with anxiety, and it’s insanely easy to get immersed in it. So, thank you Schell for putting so much effort into the games. I will forever be a fan of yours, so you can expect to see me around. For anybody that’s reading this review to see if you should buy the game, absolutely. I have no criticisms about it whatsoever, it is completely flawless.
This game is amazing, the puzzles are fun and challenging, absolutely worth the purchase if you like escape room style puzzles

reviews.review_title: This is how you do sequel's
reviews.review_description: I've played all 3 games and I'm just now writting a review for each one so this game is exactly how you do sequel's it takes the gameplay and design of the first game and just improves it in every aspect like if you've played the first or even if you didn't you will 100% like this game it's just more improved I expect you to die it's everything it's supposed to be and more

I loved the game but i can't 100% complete it because one of the Souvenirs are broke the audiophole souvenir makes the sound like i did it but says i haven done it I've reset my vr and game and just won't work. Thank you

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear that you are having trouble with this souvenir! Have you tried entering a level and then returning to the hub before checking to see if the souvenir has been unlocked for you? Please give that method a try and if it still isn't working, drop our player support team a line at  https://bit.ly/4cD3kK0 so we can help you troubleshoot the issue.

So I enjoyed the original and as a result bought the next chapter...but...I've now found I've grown tired and frankly bored by the constant restarts back from the beginning when you fail, this gameplay is reminiscent of the frustrating early days of computer gaming. Can't be bothered playing it now which is a shame.

The intro song is the best song ever!
The game is so much fun

reviews.review_description: Just as good as the original, lots of new puzzles and situations. So much fun and just te right level of difficuty. Some new features too with new collectables to get on the way. So good ;-)

reviews.review_title: For sure the best out of the 3
reviews.review_description: The best missions the best intro the best music the best story line 10/10
i love the ‘i expect you to die’ games sooooo much! the mix of story, adventure, puzzles, and comedy is truly what makes this game so memorable & special. i recommend the bundle, you won’t regret it!
This game has I think the best quality for VR games. The story is fun and engaging. It’s a one of a kind. Highly recommend getting this and the rest of the series!
Play it. Get invested. It’s worth it.
In my opinion the best game out of the series, much more story driven then the first, beautiful missions and amazing villains. My absolute favorite game of all time

reviews.review_title: Simply put. You will NOT regret this.
reviews.review_description: There are 3 games in this series. Each of the three are engaging, entertaining, funny, challenging (yet so simple you will smack yourself when you realize the answer was right in front of you.) But even delivering 3 of the very best VR Escape Room genre games, in existence, Schell wasn't satisfied...They just said "hold my beer!" Each Game has a 007 movie Theme Song based on the name of the game! JUST A TOTAL MIC DROP! Well written, well performed and they each come with an opening music video I PROMISE YOU that you will watch more than once! The only way you regret these games are if you DON'T buy them.
First game I bought never going to regret it it was so good thank you developers for making a game we love also any body who gives it less than five is just Zor agents

reviews.review_title: Play the first and third before this.
reviews.review_description: So a lot of reviews are saying they preferred this game to the first and third ones…I have no words, I think this game is certainly the worst of the series and No.3 mostly works without the context of this game, so I recommend playing them first, regardless of my distaste compared to the other two this is still totally worth the money and I highly recommend you give it a go!
i think that i expect you to die 3 should go on sale or be free.
One of my favorite out of the 3! This game is fun!

reviews.review_title: This is the greatest game ever
reviews.review_description: Look i have alot to say and it's all true the game is so much fun btw the into are sooooo good I bought the first one a while back and didn't know there was a 2nd and 3rd aperently there's a mini game but I never got it anyway I got the second and third game and played the 2 the 2nd game was easily the best the vilian used tactics and smarts in such a good way he tricked the government and nearly won the 2nd one was the only one I 100% and it's because I like replaying the games and it's really tricky the best part was the ending the thing on the elevator was pretty hard for me to figure out and you usually look past it and the three puzzles are so much fun with the adrenaline it also has my favorite intro song the best hub and the best battles the the best level is easily the the 3rd level in the 3rd game though but my second favorite is the jet if you want to play this game it's peek

I loved playing the ieytd trilogy so much I 100%ed all three games
Just so fun and love the challenges and narration

Although I personally prefer the original I Expect You To Die, this game is still pretty good and has decently challenging missions that require a lot of thinking.
Nothing's better than being a telekinetic secret agent, and this game improves on a lot from the first one! Absolutely a must-play

This game is amazing not only can you do it by yourself you can also cast the game to have help with others I loved doing this game with the help of my mother it was a fun thing for us both the graphics are 10/10 the puzzles are amazing and makes you think your a real spy.

This game is SO GOOD!!! I haven’t even played the 1st game yet they make everything so abundantly clear￼ that I don’t have to! It’s a fun puzzle game where you have to think outside the box to complete missions, and those are my FAVORITE KINDS OF GAMES!

Gameplay and graphics are pretty good, but the frustrating part is the puzzle solutions don't seem to have any logic to them. I worked out some of each level but I had to eventually keep going back to a walkthrough website which described what to do. I mean, how was I supposed to know that the 2nd and 5th daggers are what you need to put in the barrel in the wine cellar I open it? Have I missed a clue somewhere? If you need this much help to complete it, they're not good puzzles. I don't think I'll bother completing it.

I could see you, kill your stage fright, play your part right, this could be youuuuu. If all the worlds a stage... Let's set it on fireeeee, so you'll be the spy and I'll be the liarrrrre. Face to face to face again.... And through the camera lens we ascend... Your a minor part... I'm the major star HA. The credits are more than just yourrrrrrs, cause the truth is... I despise you. I will tear apart all these works of art the disguise you. I'm making the final cut... it's down to the wire... so you'll play the spy and I'll play liar... and I'll play the liar, and I'll be the liar, AND I'LL BE THE LIARRRRRRRRR
Why are parts 1 and 3 with Spanish texts and this second part doesn't have them? Please add the texts in Spanish so that my parents who do not know English can enjoy this title as well.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! While we are working to update as many of our games with subtitles, you are correct; regrettably not all of our titles currently have this feature. We're sorry we don't have more to share at this time.  We've documented your request for additional languages and will consider it as we plan for additional development on our games across the board.

EDIT: Just finished. Absolutely great. Also, there are always clues, the puzzles are not random so don't give up! Just chill and enjoy the vibes while you fiddle with stuff and explore.

This game is HEAVILY SCRIPTED. You have to follow a very specific set of steps to solve each puzzle, none of which are at all intuitive, and the penalty for making a wrong step is DEATH, at which point you have to START ALL OVER AGAIN. This is NOT FUN. It is boring, repetitive, and tedious as hell.

This sucks

Couldn't even start...
First of all, why the long credits at the beginning? We just want to start playing. It adds Zero value to it, it's just boring, no one will read any names and music sux.
After the useless credits end, by mistake inserted the tape of the first part, so it started again from the beginning and there's just no way of skipping it. There's no menu, nothing. If I have to redo the beginning and watch those credits again, I won't even bother opening it again. Keep my money, it's ok.
Maybe one day if I am extremely bored I will play and see how it is. Then if justified, I will change my review.

developer_response.body:
Greetings, Agent! We're sorry to hear you didn't enjoy the opening credits sequence. Hopefully you'll give it a second chance after completing the game.

That said, if you truly want to skip it, it is completely possible. If you press the menu button on the left controller, you'll bring up the pause menu during the credits. There will be an option called 'Skip Credits' so you can continue on to the van and start your first mission by inserting the tape for Operation: Stage Fright.

Stay sharp, Agent.

reviews.review_description: Well, this game is pretty terrible, ULTRA hard and raging… can’t get passed the first level? I have played so much VR, but this is just awful, the tutorial doesn’t help at all. I have been able to win so much in Beat Saber and Superhot VR but this is absolutely not worth it.

This game is incredible,  the puzzles can range from "this is so easy" to "dear God, what the hell do I do?" It's incredible. You'll be panicking to save your life and others lives to the point where the level is finished, you're legitimately letting out a breath of relief and leaning back in your chair, like you just saved your life. Its freaking amazing!

Amazing graphics, immersive interactions and excellent storytelling. Really worth the money. I wish there would be a turning mechanism so I can play this while seated on my bed - sometimes there’s stuff behind you that you need to interact with and that becomes a challenge when you have a wall behind you :)

From the bottom of my heart I fee I must rebalance this game's rating.

If you're looking for a clever game, well it's NOT you want to buy.

This game came as a suggestion from an Escape Room enthusiast group. It did not disappoint. This was the first game I purchased, and will continue to look for similar games.  I used walkthrough guides to get me by a few times, which may not have been necessary if I didn’t have the patience of a toddler. While you can die, it isn’t graphic. There are no jump scares (with the exception of one operation) so it is still a good game for the self-proclaimed scaredy-cat. I hope they come out with a 3rd.
